commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=4075ef969dde283d5e52103b7229b9e15367ab01)
commands.review_summary:
The PR disables startup progress for `--json`, adds a stdout guard before creating the Clack spinner, and adds a progress unit test for piped stdout suppression.

PR surface: Source +7, Tests +36. Total +43 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source and dependency inspection give a high-confidence reproduction path: current main creates a default Clack spinner before JSON output, and Clack defaults that spinner to stdout. I did not run the full OpenClaw CLI because this read-only review avoided dependency install/build artifacts.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor-visible code/test changes plus real behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not repair around the missing external proof gate.

target.title: fix(cli): suppress startup progress spinner for --json and piped stdout
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem**: openclaw sessions --json emits Clack spinner/progress glyphs before the JSON payload, breaking jq and script parsers. Issue #88602.
- **Root Cause**: @clack/prompts spinner() writes terminal control sequences directly to process.stdout. The CLI startup progress creates a spinner even when --json is passed or stdout is piped, causing glyphs to leak into structured output.
- **Fix**: Two layers of defense:
  1. src/cli/progress.ts: Only create a clack spinner when process.stdout.isTTY is not explicitly false.
  2. src/cli/run-main.ts: Disable startup progress entirely when --json is detected via hasJsonOutputFlag().
- **What changed**:
  - src/cli/progress.ts: added stdoutSafe guard before creating spinner()
  - src/cli/run-main.ts: passed enabled: !hasJsonOutputFlag(normalizedArgv)
  - src/cli/progress.test.ts: new test for non-TTY stdout suppression
- **What did NOT change**: OSC-based progress, line mode, log fallback, and fallback:none path.

## Verification

- Verified clack spinner uses process.stdout.write() irrespective of the configured progress stream.
- New unit test confirms zero stream writes when stdout is not a TTY.
- Manual: openclaw sessions --json now outputs clean JSON without leading glyphs.

Fixes #88602
